Road Knights Motorcycle Club.description: An outlaw motorcycle club based in the South Island of New Zealand, founded in 1979. It operates chapters in Invercargill, Dunedin, and Timaru, and has expanded to Thailand, Netherlands, and the United States since 2012. The club has been involved in violent conflicts with rival gangs such as the Mongrel Mob and the Damned. As of 2009, membership in New Zealand was low and former leadership had dispersed, died, or gone to jail.

descriptions: Hells Angels are allied with Road Knights, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: New Zealand's Hells Angels are allied with the Head Hunters and the Road Knights.

descriptions: Road Knights listed as rival of Mongrel Mob in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Black Power, Road Knights, Tribesmen, and Killer Beez

descriptions: Road Knights and Damned were at war in the late 1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1980s, the Road Knights became involved in a war with the Damned which resulted in several violent incidents.

descriptions: Road Knights and Mongrel Mob are rivals, with arson and theft in 2008.
edges.evidence_quote: Its rivalry with the Mongrel Mob boiled over in June 2008 in Invercargill when the Road Knights' building was burned down and two motorcycles stolen.

descriptions: The La Torre clan is a subgroup of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Camorra's organizational structure is divided into individual groups called 'clans'.

Hudson Dusters.description: The Hudson Dusters were a New York City street gang active from the late 1890s to 1917. Formed by Circular Jack, Kid Yorke, and Goo Goo Knox, they operated from Hudson Street in Manhattan. The gang controlled much of Manhattan's West Side and the Hudson River docks, engaging in burglary, robbery, assault, and narcotics. They were allied with the Gopher Gang and had ties to Tammany Hall.

descriptions: The Boodle Gang and Hudson Dusters were rivals, as listed in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Potashes , Hudson Dusters

descriptions: Hudson Dusters displaced Boodle Gang from dominance in Lower West Side Manhattan.
edges.evidence_quote: They and the much older Boodle Gang dominated Lower West Side Manhattan until the end of the 1890s when they were displaced by the then emerging Hudson Dusters

descriptions: The Five Points Gang was strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters.
edges.evidence_quote: The Five Points Gang (led by Paul Kelly) would rise to prominence during the early 1900s, strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters, the Gopher Gang, and others during the period.

Osmanen Germania.description: Turkish-nationalistic and extreme right criminal gang in Germany, formed between late 2014 and April 2015. It was involved in organized crime including assault, blackmail, prostitution, kidnapping, narcotics, and gun crime. The gang had connections to the Turkish AKP party and the neo-fascist Grey Wolves, and was banned by the German government in 2018.

descriptions: Osmanen Germania BC worked as security guards for the Grey Wolves from 2014 to 2018.
edges.evidence_quote: From 2014-2018 the Osmanen Germania were an extreme right criminal gang working as security guards for the gray wolves.

descriptions: AKP financially supported Osmanen Germania.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was said to be financially supported by the Turkish ruling party AKP

descriptions: Osmanen Germania had violent clashes with Bahoz.

descriptions: Osmanen Germania had connections with the Grey Wolves.
edges.evidence_quote: It had connections to the neo-fascist Grey Wolves.

descriptions: Osmanen Germania's expansion into red-light districts increases likelihood of territorial battle with Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The newspaper Hannoversche Allgemeine Zeitung claimed that the Osmanen Germania was advancing more and more into the red-light districts , which increases the likelihood of a bloody territorial battle with established gangs like the Hells Angels Motorcycle Club and the Mongols Motorcycle Club .

descriptions: Osmanen Germania's expansion into red-light districts increases likelihood of territorial battle with Mongols.

Muslim Brotherhood.description: Transnational Sunni Islamist organization founded in Egypt in 1928 by Hassan al-Banna. Combines political activism with Islamic charity work, aiming to establish a state ruled by Sharia under a caliphate. It gained political power after the Arab Spring but was overthrown in 2013 and subsequently banned and designated a terrorist group in Egypt and several other countries.

descriptions: Hamas emerged from a charity affiliated with the Muslim Brotherhood.
edges.evidence_quote: It emerged from his 1973 Mujama al-Islamiya Islamic charity affiliated with the Muslim Brotherhood.

descriptions: Hamas emerged from the Muslim Brotherhood-affiliated charity.

Individualists Tending to the Wild.description: Self-defined eco-extremist group that emerged in Mexico in 2011. It opposes civilization and technological progress, advocating for a return to primitive hunter-gatherer lifestyles. The group has claimed responsibility for letter bombs and other attacks, though many claims have been disputed by authorities. Its ideology is inspired by Ted Kaczynski and includes elements of ecofascism and neo-Luddism.

edges.evidence_quote: In a statement the self-styled "Sociedad Salvaje Silvestre" cell took responsibility for the attack

edges.evidence_quote: A leader of the ITS claimed to have been influenced by the satanic fascist group the Tempel ov Blood .

'Ndrina Dragone.description: The 'Ndrina Dragone is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cutro, Calabria, Italy. Founded by Antonio Dragone in the 1960s, it engaged in extortion, robbery, and murder. The clan was involved in a long-running feud with the Oliverio family and later with the Grande Aracri and Nicoscia clans. It expanded into Emilia-Romagna in the 1980s, establishing criminal interests in Reggio Emilia, Modena, and Piacenza. The clan's power declined after the killing of Antonio Dragone in 2004 and subsequent police operations.

descriptions: Dragone is listed as an ally of Arena.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Dragone

descriptions: Dragone is a subunit of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Dragone è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese

descriptions: Dragone is allied with Arabia.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata con ... gli Arabia di Cutro

descriptions: Dragone is allied with Arena.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata con ... gli Arena di Isola Capo Rizzuto

descriptions: Dragone and Grande Aracri were opposing sides in the Faida di Cutro.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Cutro: combattuta tra la fine degli anni '90 e i primi del 2000, ha visto scontrarsi i Dragone e i Grande Aracri

descriptions: Dragone and Grande Aracri were in conflict during the Cutro feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 10 maggio 2004 viene ucciso Antonio Dragone e in seguito il suo genero Gaetano Ciampà . La faida riprende nel 2004 con l'omicidio di Franco Arena scoppia una faida fra gli Arena -Dragone e i Nicoscia - Grande Aracri

descriptions: Dragone and Grande Aracri made a mafia peace in 2025.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 12 marzo del 2025 scatta l’operazione Ten. Si tratta di una “pace mafiosa” tra i Dragone e I Grande Aracri

descriptions: Dragone is allied with Mannolo.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata con i Mannolo di San Leonardo di Cutro

descriptions: Dragone and Oliverio were in a feud in 1972.
edges.evidence_quote: faida nel 1972 che portò all'omicidio di: Francesco Dragone, Antonio Spagnolo, Vittorio Colacino e Gaetano, Domenico e Salvatore Oliverio

descriptions: Dragone is allied with Trapasso-Zoffreo.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: ... Trapasso-Zoffreo

descriptions: Dragone and Vasapollo were in a feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Ne scoppia una anche tra i Dragone e i Vasapollo

descriptions: Grande Aracri split from Dragone, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: A violent split from the Dragone 'ndrina led to multiple murders and a long-lasting internal feud.

descriptions: Grande Aracri was in a faida (conflict) with the Dragone family.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1999 scoppia la faida con l'omicidio di Raffaele Dragone

descriptions: Outlaws and Avenger MC members were together during a fatal incident, but no explicit relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: On February 24, 2001, several members of the Dayton Outlaws chapter, along with members of the Avenger Motorcycle Club, visited Spanky's Dollhouse...

descriptions: Zemmour crime family is a defunct component of the Corsican mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Zemmour crime family (defunct)

Axe Gang.description: The Axe Gang was a militant organisation active in Shanghai and surrounding areas of the Republic of China during the early 20th century. Originating among Anhui migrant workers, it evolved from labour mobilisation into a faction involved in political violence and assassinations amid the Warlord Era and early Nationalist period. Its most prominent leader was Wang Yaqiao. The gang declined after Wang's assassination in 1936.

descriptions: The Green Gang absorbed or eliminated the Axe Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: With the consolidation of Nationalist control and the strengthening of larger syndicates such as the Green Gang, smaller independent militant groups like the Axe Gang were gradually eliminated or absorbed.

descriptions: The Axe Gang formed connections with the New Guangxi clique.
edges.evidence_quote: Wang's organisation maintained its relevance by forming connections with other regional military factions, including elements of the New Guangxi clique.

Clan dei marsigliesi.description: Mafia-style criminal organization founded in Rome in 1973, operating between France and Italy during the first half of the 1970s. Engaged in drug trafficking and lucrative kidnappings, becoming a major crime industry and exerting territorial control in Rome. Composed of French criminals from the Marseille milieu, linked to the French Connection.

descriptions: Danilo Abbruciati entered the gambling dens controlled by the Clan dei marsigliesi, indicating ties.
edges.evidence_quote: per poi entrare nel giro delle bische clandestine controllate dal Clan dei marsigliesi di Albert Bergamelli

descriptions: The arrival of the Clan dei marsigliesi changed the power dynamics in Rome's underworld, preceding the Banda della Magliana.
edges.evidence_quote: con l'avvento del clan dei marsigliesi ... si determinò un deciso cambiamento dei rapporti di forze all'interno della piccola e frammentata malavita capitolina

descriptions: Camorra clans, allied with Sicilian mafia, defeated the Marseille clan.
edges.evidence_quote: i clan camorristici, in combutta con i mafiosi siciliani, riuscirono a sbaragliare la concorrenza straniera del clan dei marsigliesi

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana succeeded the Clan dei marsigliesi after its decline.
edges.evidence_quote: La successiva unione di alcune di queste batterie diede poi vita a una vera e propria banda per il controllo totale dei traffici illeciti romani conosciuta come Banda della Magliana .

descriptions: The Clan dei marsigliesi originated from members of the French Connection milieu.
edges.evidence_quote: provenienti in gran parte dal cosiddetto milieu marsigliese , il cartello criminale francese meglio conosciuto come " French Connection "

descriptions: Investigations looked into possible contacts with the Marseille clan regarding gold smuggling.
edges.evidence_quote: per tale supposizione l'apertura di un ulteriore filone investigativo per verificare possibili contatti con il clan dei marsigliesi

descriptions: Cosa Nostra took over heroin trafficking from the Marseille clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Cosa nostra, sfruttando le direttrici tradizionali del contrabbando di sigarette, rilevò gran parte del traffico di eroina fino ad allora appannaggio esclusivo del clan dei marsigliesi

descriptions: Abate clan was an ally of Vollaro clan (now defunct).
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Ascione clan, Abate clan (defunct), Giuliano clan (defunct), Anastasio clan, Veneruso clan, Marfella clan

Bottoms Gang.description: The Bottoms Gang was an American street gang based in St. Louis, Missouri, active from around 1905 to 1917. Founded by Frank Hussey and Anthony Foley, the gang engaged in voter intimidation, armed robbery, assault, illegal lottery, and murder. Primarily Irish-American, they were known for extreme violence and feuded with the larger Egan's Rats gang and the St. Louis Police Department. The gang ceased to exist by the time the United States entered World War I.

descriptions: Surviving Bottoms Gang members joined the Chouteau Avenue Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: A handful of the surviving members had relocated to the Soulard district where they fell in with a local mob known as the Chouteau Avenue Gang.

descriptions: The Bottoms Gang feuded with Egan's Rats.
edges.evidence_quote: They feuded with the larger Egan's Rats gang

descriptions: The Nixie Fighters were a splinter group of the Bottoms Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: A splinter group of the Bottoms Gang became known as the 'Nixie Fighters'

descriptions: The Bottoms Gang was a rival of Egan's Rats, and they engaged in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The Egan Gang's main rival at this time was the violent Bottoms Gang from the Twenty-second Ward.

descriptions: FARC 48th Front is listed as an ally of Los Lobos.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Los Tiguerones, Los Chone Killers, Kompania Bello, FARC 48th Front, Tren de Aragua

descriptions: CGM is listed as a rival of Mali Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: CGM

Los Rastrojos.description: Colombian narcoparamilitary organized armed group formed by dissidents of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC) after its demobilization in 2006. It became independent after the assassination of its main founder in Venezuela in 2008 and was one of the most important drug trafficking organizations in Colombia. The group finances itself through cocaine, marijuana, and heroin trafficking, as well as illegal gold mining. It operated mainly in Valle del Cauca and Cali, with influence in other parts of Colombia and western Venezuela. By 2017, the group had fragmented into small factions.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos formed from former AUC members who refused demobilization, indicating a successor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Los GAO y GDO se formaron como consecuencia a la negativa de antiguos miembros de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC) al proceso de desmovilización

descriptions: Los Rastrojos bought the Bloque Meta franchise in 2013.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rastrojos habrían comprado en 2013 la franquicia del Bloque Meta

descriptions: Cachiros is allied with Los Rastrojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Los Rastrojos , Norte del Valle Cartel , Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Los Rastrojos was formed by Norte del Valle Cartel capo Wilber Varela.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rastrojos is a Colombian drug trafficking paramilitary organization . The group was formed by Norte del Valle cartel capo Wilber Varela, alias 'Jabon' and one of his right-hand men, 'Diego Rastrojo', around 2004 when Varela fell out with fellow-capo Diego Leon Montoya, alias ' Don Diego '.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos is a faction of the Cartel del Norte del Valle led by Wilber Alirio Varela.
edges.evidence_quote: Wilber Alirio Varela con sus aliados y su ejército personal que denominó " Los Rastrojos ".

descriptions: The AGC declared war on Los Rastrojos for control of drug trade.
edges.evidence_quote: In late 2011, the AGC declared war on Los Rastrojos over the control of the drug trade in Medellín.

descriptions: The text describes them as the two main groups that expanded territorially, implying competition.
edges.evidence_quote: El Clan Úsuga y Los Rastrojos eran reconocidas como las dos grandes agrupaciones que han logrado una expansión territorial considerable...

descriptions: Clan del Golfo was formerly a rival of Los Rastrojos.
edges.evidence_quote: su otrora rival "Clan del Golfo"

descriptions: Clan del Golfo had a strong rivalry with Los Rastrojos.
edges.evidence_quote: La agrupación mantenía una fuerte rivalidad con otras bandas emergentes como Los Rastrojos

descriptions: Clan del Golfo had a strong rivalry with Los Rastrojos over territory and drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: La agrupación mantenía una fuerte rivalidad con otras bandas emergentes entre las que figuran Los Rastrojos y la Oficina de Envigado .

descriptions: Los Rastrojos is described as a rival criminal group to Clan del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: poco antes de la detención de Téllez, un exmiembro del grupo criminal rival Los Rastrojos

descriptions: Clan del Golfo and Los Rastrojos dispute control of illegal mining.
edges.evidence_quote: El control total de la minería ilegal ... fue el motivo principal del ataque, un negocio que también disputan Los Rastrojos

descriptions: Clan del Golfo and Los Rastrojos held a meeting in 2012 to agree on territory division and future alliance.

descriptions: Los Rastrojos operate in the same zones as Clan del Golfo.

descriptions: Clan del Golfo, Los Rastrojos, and Los Paisas held a secret meeting in 2012 to end conflicts and work together.
edges.evidence_quote: representantes del Clan Úsuga, al igual que de los desaparecidos Rastrojos y Paisas, habrían sostenido una reunión clandestina en 2012 en la ciudad de Medellín , para acordar una repartición de "territorios" a nivel nacional y así acabar con los enfrentamientos entre las mismas organizaciones criminales y comenzar a trabajar en conjunto para una futura alianza.
